items.title: EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy
items.summary: EchoStar subsidiaries tied to its satellite TV and abandoned 5G network businesses have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, advancing a prepackaged restructuring plan to repay debt early after selling spectrum to SpaceX and AT&T. The post EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy...

items.title: US lifts export curbs on Anthropic AI models
items.summary: Anthropic resumed the rollout of its controversial Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models after the US Commerce Department lifted export controls imposed earlier this month over cybersecurity concerns. The post US lifts export curbs on Anthropic AI models appeared first on Mobile World Live .

items.title: CMA fast-tracks probe into £2B fibre deal
items.summary: The UK’s Competition and Markets Authority launched an in-depth probe into Nexfibre’s planned £2 billion takeover of fellow fibre broadband player Netomnia, putting a question mark on potential consolidation in the sector. The post CMA fast-tracks probe into £2B fibre deal appeared first on Mobile World Live .

items.title: Zain wins 25-year Syria telecoms licence for $750M
items.summary: Zain Group secured a 25-year telecoms licence in Syria after winning a tender process with a bid totalling $747 million, replacing MTN’s existing network in the country. The post Zain wins 25-year Syria telecoms licence for $750M appeared first on Mobile World Live .

items.title: FCC to vote on plan for 160MHz upper C-Band auction
items.summary: US Federal Communications Commission chair Brendan Carr revealed the agency would vote on an order setting up an auction of 160MHz of spectrum in the upper C-Band covering 3.98GHz to 4.14GHz next month, with the sale process scheduled for 2027. items.title: AWS launches $1B engineering unit for AI
items.summary: Amazon Web Services created a dedicated forward deployed engineering organisation, designed to embed its engineers directly inside customer teams to build and deploy production AI systems, compressing deployment timelines from months to days. items.title: Ericsson cloud boss still resists a Nokia move in the 5G core
items.summary: Unlike Nokia, Ericsson continues to work on its own cloud infrastructure platform, and that is not about to change, says Jenny Lindqvist, who heads up Ericsson's cloud software and services group.

items.title: Direct-to-Cell Technology: Enabling Satellite Connectivity for Legacy Devices
items.summary: Direct-to-cell technology uses LEO satellites as spaceborne cell towers. It delivers LTE services to existing smartphones without hardware changes, bridging global coverage gaps. What Attendees will Learn How DTC works as a spaceborne cell tower — LEO satellites carry LTE eNodeB payloads in regenerative mode. How they...

items.title: Starlink and Amazon Could Gain Access to Europe’s Satellite Airwaves
items.summary: The European Union plans to reserve most future satellite spectrum for regional firms while still allowing Starlink and Amazon to compete, reflecting Brussels' attempt to balance technological sovereignty with market openness in a strategically sensitive communications sector.

items.title: RF Digital Twins: Why 5G-Advanced and 6G Need Predictive Simulation

items.title: 6G-Enabled Autonomous Systems Take Center Stage at NetRobiCS 2026
items.summary: The 19th International Workshop on Networked Robotics and Communication Systems (NetRobiCS 2026) successfully concluded yesterday in Tokyo, Japan, with the one6G Association serving as a key technical sponsor. Held as part of the IEEE INFOCOM 2026 conference, […] The post 6G-Enabled Autonomous Systems Take Center...

items.title: Can Jio build India's answer to Starlink?
items.summary: India’s largest service provider, Reliance Jio, is evaluating the development of a sovereign low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellation, which would challenge several global satellite broadband providers, including Starlink, Eutelsat OneWeb and Amazon’s Project LEO, among others.

items.title: T-Mobile says older-plan customers need 5G refresh
items.summary: Some T-Mobile customers are being notified today that they’re getting moved to newer, sometimes pricier, rate plans.

items.title: SpaceX plans bigger Starlink mobile push in US: report
items.summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX may be coming for mobile phone companies faster than you can say “Can you hear me now?”

items.title: Rocket Lab acquires Iridium in $8B deal
items.summary: Rocket Lab Corporation is acquiring Iridium Communications in a deal valued at $8 billion, uniting Rocket Lab’s launch and satellite manufacturing capabilities with Iridium’s global satellite network and L-band spectrum.

items.title: SDN pioneers return with $15M GPU networking bet
items.summary: A16z leads a $15M Series A to upstart Netris, funding a platform automating GPU networking and multi-tenancy for AI cloud operators.

items.title: FCC tees up big auction for C-band ‘super band’
items.summary: The industry just finished a little AWS-3 auction and now it’s gearing up for a much bigger one: 160 megahertz in the upper C-band next year.

items.title: Now SpaceX is talking to Charter about a mobile offer: report
items.summary: The latest buzz is that Elon Musk’s SpaceX held executive-level talks with Charter Communications about partnering on a consumer mobile phone offering, per a Friday Bloomberg report.

items.title: M&A tracker - Verizon, BT, Iridium, Rocket Lab and Comcast
items.summary: It's increasingly difficult to stay on top of the ever-changing business landscape the underpins the telecom and tech industries. We're here to help!

items.title: Huawei and partners launch OpenAN project for Level 4 autonomous networks
items.summary: The project delivers a set of out-of-the-box open-source components targeting the accelerated delivery of smart link, agile build, large-scale collaboration and deterministic execution of telecom agents.

items.title: CoreWeave launches ARIA to boost AI-enabled research
items.summary: ARIA is an AI research agent that reads experiment data, uncovers hidden insights, and drives continuous model and agent improvement.

items.title: Update: Verizon, BT merge international enterprise operations in 50:50 JV worth $4B
items.summary: The JV will create a new global connectivity company focused on multinational organizations and serve 3,000+ businesses.

items.title: Verizon emerges as biggest winner in AWS-3 auction
items.summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) concluded Auction 113 last week and announced the winners on Friday, with Verizon emerging as the biggest spender.
